prerequisites: A running Redis server (the connector supports Redis 2.8.x, 3.x.x, and above) reachable from Airbyte, with its hostname/address and port.

A Redis user with permission to write data, plus that user's username and password if authentication is enabled (Redis 6+ supports per-user ACL credentials; older servers use the default user with a requirepass password).
A decision on which Redis data type to store records as via cache_type (for example hash, set, list, or stream).
If connecting over TLS: set ssl and, for verify-full, the CA certificate and any client certificate, client key, and client key password.
If the server is not directly reachable: SSH bastion host details for tunnel_method (SSH host, user, and either an SSH key or a tunnel user password).

authMethods.name: Username and password (Redis AUTH / ACL)
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with the Redis server using the username and password config fields. On Redis 6+ this maps to an ACL user authenticated via AUTH <username> <password>; on the default user the password can be set with requirepass, which is equivalent to ACL SETUSER default ><password>. The password is stored in the connector's secret password field.

authMethods.name: TLS client certificate (ssl_mode verify-full)
authMethods.summary: When ssl is enabled with ssl_mode set to verify-full, the connection is encrypted and the server identity is verified. Supply the CA certificate (ssl_mode.ca_certificate) and, for mutual TLS, the client certificate, client key, and client key password, all held as secret fields.

authMethods.name: SSH tunnel
authMethods.summary: Optionally reach the Redis server through an SSH bastion using tunnel_method. Authenticate to the bastion with either an SSH private key (tunnel_method.ssh_key) or a tunnel user password (tunnel_method.tunnel_user_password), both stored as secrets.

setupSteps.title: Confirm Redis server connectivity
setupSteps.body: Ensure your Redis server is running and reachable from Airbyte. Note its hostname or address (host) and port. The connector is compatible with Redis 2.8.x, 3.x.x and above.

setupSteps.title: Prepare Redis credentials
setupSteps.body: Set up the username and password used to authenticate with the Redis server. On Redis 6+, create or use an ACL user (for example with ACL SETUSER) and provide that user's name and password. For the default user you can set its password via requirepass. Provide these as the username and password config fields.

setupSteps.title: Choose the cache type
setupSteps.body: Set cache_type to the Redis data type the connector should use when storing incoming records, for example hash, set, list, or stream.

setupSteps.title: Configure encryption (optional)
setupSteps.body: To encrypt the connection, enable ssl and set ssl_mode. Use verify-full to require encryption with server identity verification, supplying the CA certificate and, for mutual TLS, the client certificate, client key, and client key password. Use disable for no encryption.

setupSteps.title: Configure SSH tunnel (optional)
setupSteps.body: If the Redis server is not directly accessible, configure tunnel_method to connect through an SSH bastion. Authenticate to the bastion with either an SSH key or the tunnel user password.

setupSteps.title: Save and test the destination
setupSteps.body: Enter the host, port, username, password, and cache_type (plus any SSL or SSH tunnel settings) in the Airbyte destination configuration, then save and run the connection test to verify the connector can reach and write to Redis.
